2605.23215.title: FastKernels: Benchmarking GPU Kernel Generation in Production
2605.23215.paper_summary: LLM-based agents for GPU kernel generation are advancing rapidly, yet their progress is fundamentally constrained by the benchmarks they optimize against. Existing benchmarks are poorly aligned with production inference frameworks: they evaluate kernels on a single GPU with synthetic inputs, ignore the surrounding compilation stack, and reward replicating known optimizations rather than discovering new ones. The resulting reward signals are misleading: agents learn to generate kernels that score well in sandboxes but introduce interface incompatibilities, compilation-stack conflicts, and silent correctness degradation when integrated into real systems. We introduce FastKernels, a kernel benchmark built around a minimal set of 46 representative architectures spanning 8 categories, whose kernels collectively subsume those of 96.2% (409/425) of HuggingFace Transformers architectures. FastKernels doubles as a minimalistic, production-grade inference framework that runs at parity with hardened systems such as vLLM and SGLang on mainstream LLM serving and substantially exceeds upstream references on under-served architectures; each task's interface mirrors the corresponding module in the state-of-the-art library for its architecture family, enabling direct deployment of optimized kernels into production codebases. Evaluating state-of-the-art kernel agents on FastKernels, we find that even the strongest agent achieves only 0.94$\times$ aggregate speedup over production baselines, with weaker agents at $0.78\times$ and $0.53\times$ -- confirming that benchmark-production misalignment is a critical bottleneck for the field. We release FastKernels as a stepping stone toward kernel agents whose benchmark gains translate directly into production throughput improvements. Code is available at https://github.com/Snowflake-AI-Research/fastkernels
2605.23215.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基於代理的 GPU 核心生成正在快速進展，但其進展根本上受到其優化的基準的限制。現有的基準與生產推理框架之間的對齊不佳：它們在單個 GPU 上以合成輸入評估核心，忽略了周圍的編譯堆棧，並獎勵複製已知的優化而非發現新的優化。由此產生的獎勵信號具有誤導性：代理學會生成在沙盒中得分良好的核心，但在整合到實際系統時引入介面不兼容、編譯堆棧衝突和靜默的正確性下降。我們推出了 FastKernels，一個圍繞 46 種代表性架構的最小基準，涵蓋 8 個類別，其核心集合包含了 96.2%（409/425）HuggingFace Transformers 架構的核心。FastKernels 同時作為一個簡約的生產級推理框架，其性能與 vLLM 和 SGLang 等加固系統在主流 LLM 服務上相當，並在服務不足的架構上大幅超過上游參考；每個任務的介面與其架構系列的最先進庫中的相應模塊相鏡像，使得優化核心可以直接部署到生產代碼庫中。在 FastKernels 上評估最先進的核心代理，我們發現即使是最強的代理也僅實現了相對於生產基準的 0.94$\times$ 總體加速，較弱的代理則為 $0.78\times$ 和 $0.53\times$ — 確認基準與生產的不對齊是該領域的一個關鍵瓶頸。我們發布 FastKernels 作為邁向核心代理的一個基石，這些代理的基準增益可以直接轉化為生產吞吐量的改進。代碼可在 https://github.com/Snowflake-AI-Research/fastkernels 獲得。
2605.23215.author: Gabriele Oliaro et.al.
2605.23215.authors: Gabriele Oliaro, Yichao Fu, May Jiang, Owen Lu, Junli Wang, Zhihao Jia, Hao Zhang, Samyam Rajbhandari
